description: Add OpenCode and Codex as model providers to RalphDash alongside the existing Claude provider

userStories.title: Add envVarName to ProviderConfig type
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ProviderConfig type to include an optional envVarName field so OpenCode can store which environment variable name to use for injecting the API key.
acceptanceCriteria: ProviderConfig in backend/src/providers/types.ts has a new optional field: envVarName?: string
Existing Claude provider is unaffected (field is optional, Claude doesn't use it)
sanitizeProviderConfig in backend/src/routes/models.ts is updated to pass through envVarName (not a secret — it's the variable name, not the value)
Typecheck passes (cd backend && npm run build)

userStories.title: Create Codex provider backend implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Codex provider class that implements the Provider interface so the system can build the correct environment and CLI args when running Codex projects.
acceptanceCriteria: New file backend/src/providers/codex.ts implementing the Provider interface
name is 'codex'
getEnvVars() injects the stored token as both CODEX_API_KEY and OPENAI_API_KEY simultaneously
getCliArgs() returns ['--full-auto'] for auto-approve
getModelVariants() returns exactly: ['gpt-5.3-codex', 'gpt-5.2-codex', 'gpt-5.1-codex-max', 'gpt-5.2', 'gpt-5.1-codex-mini']
getFilesToSync() returns an empty array
parseConfig() reads token and model from raw config JSON and returns a normalized ProviderConfig
Provider is registered in backend/src/providers/registry.ts
Typecheck passes (cd backend && npm run build)

userStories.title: Create OpenCode provider backend implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an OpenCode provider class that implements the Provider interface so the system can build the correct environment and CLI args when running OpenCode projects.
acceptanceCriteria: New file backend/src/providers/opencode.ts implementing the Provider interface
name is 'opencode'
getEnvVars() injects the stored token using the user-specified envVarName from config (e.g., OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...)
getCliArgs() returns ['--yolo'] for auto-approve
getModelVariants() returns an empty array (OpenCode uses free-text model input, not a fixed list)
getFilesToSync() returns an empty array
parseConfig() reads token, model, and envVarName from raw config JSON and returns a normalized ProviderConfig
Provider is registered in backend/src/providers/registry.ts
Typecheck passes (cd backend && npm run build)

userStories.title: Update models API routes for new providers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the models API to handle OpenCode and Codex provider configuration — saving tokens, models, and env var names — without the Claude-specific sk-ant-* token validation.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /api/models/:provider/token accepts tokens for opencode and codex providers with non-empty-string-only validation (no sk-ant-* prefix check)
PUT /api/models/:provider/token for opencode also accepts an envVarName field in the request body and stores it in the provider config
PUT /api/models/:provider/model works for codex (validates against its modelVariants array) and for opencode (accepts any non-empty string since it has no fixed variant list)
Provider DB rows are created lazily on first PUT if they don't already exist (instead of being seeded on startup)
sanitizeProviderConfig includes envVarName in sanitized output for the frontend
GET /api/models always returns all registered providers, even if not yet configured (with is_configured: false)
Typecheck passes (cd backend && npm run build)

userStories.title: Add OpenCode tab to Models page
userStories.description: As a user, I want an OpenCode tab on the Models page so I can configure OpenCode as a provider with my API key, env var name, and model.
acceptanceCriteria: Models page tabs include 'OpenCode' alongside 'Claude'
OpenCode tab has an Authentication section with: a text input for the environment variable name (e.g., OPENAI_API_KEY), a password input for the API key value, and a Save button that stores both
A 'Configured' badge is shown when is_configured is true
OpenCode tab has a Model section with: a free-text input for the model identifier (no dropdown), a Save button, and current model shown in a badge when set
OpenCode tab does NOT have an Auto Memory preferences section
Typecheck passes (cd frontend && npm run build)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Allow assigning projects to new providers
userStories.description: As a user, I want to assign a project to use OpenCode or Codex so that runs use the correct CLI tool and credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /api/projects/:id/provider accepts 'opencode' and 'codex' as valid provider values
When switching a project to OpenCode, model_variant is set to null (no default)
When switching a project to Codex, model_variant is set to 'gpt-5.3-codex'
PUT /api/projects/:id/model-variant for OpenCode projects accepts any non-empty string (not validated against a variants list)
The project provider dropdown on the Dashboard includes OpenCode and Codex as options
Typecheck passes (cd frontend && npm run build)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Run projects with OpenCode and Codex providers
userStories.description: As a user, I want to start a run on a project assigned to OpenCode or Codex so the correct CLI is invoked with the right environment variables and flags.
acceptanceCriteria: processManager.startRun() works for OpenCode projects: spawns the runner script with OpenCode env vars (user-specified env var name -> token) and --yolo flag
processManager.startRun() works for Codex projects: spawns the runner script with both CODEX_API_KEY and OPENAI_API_KEY set, and --full-auto flag
Runs fail gracefully with a clear error message if the provider is not configured (no token saved)
Typecheck passes (cd backend && npm run build)

userStories.title: Add Codex tab to Models page
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Codex tab on the Models page so I can configure Codex as a provider with my API key and select a model.
acceptanceCriteria: Models page tabs include 'Codex' alongside 'Claude' and 'OpenCode'
Codex tab has an Authentication section with: a password input for the API key, a Save button, and a 'Configured' badge when is_configured is true. No env var name input (Codex uses fixed var names)

Codex tab has a Model section with: a dropdown containing exactly gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5.2-codex, gpt-5.1-codex-max, gpt-5.2, gpt-5.1-codex-mini, a Save button, and current model shown in a badge when set
Codex tab does NOT have an Auto Memory preferences section
Typecheck passes (cd frontend && npm run build)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create placeholder runner scripts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need placeholder runner scripts for OpenCode and Codex so the process manager has valid script paths to invoke.
acceptanceCriteria: Create empty placeholder script scripts/ralph/ralph-opencode.sh
Create empty placeholder script scripts/ralph/ralph-codex.sh
Both scripts are executable (chmod +x)
These scripts will be filled in with actual logic in a future task
